Quickly, seven keys to joint ventures, strategic allotments, to accessing other people's customers in any way, shape or form that you would do it. They are not necessarily in priority or you don't have this in your manual. One and they don't all necessarily apply to every circumstance. One is the other party's poor use of their customers or customer relationships or under estimation of its value. So in many cases, they got good customers, they're under exploiting them, they're under using them, they're under serving them and or they don't even understand the value of what they got. So in some cases that makes the job harder because you're dealing with a dummy. In other cases though, it makes the job easier because they're not getting any significant revenue at all on their back end and you have an opportunity to provide it to them. The best situation is underserved, under marketed to or poorly marketed to customers who stubbornly are good anyway. So like I always love going after Nightingale Conant customers because they're good buyers from bad marketing. Let me add them. Number two, incredible laziness on the other person's part or if you wish to be kind, the other person is just incredibly busy and overwhelmed. The reason that that's an opportunity is because most people who come to them with opportunities, their things to do, put a lot of the work of doing it on their plate. I want to sell my stuff to your guys but I don't have a sales ladder, it's worth a shit. Oh jeez, now we got to write a sales ladder. I don't have any good testimonials. Crap, we got to teach this guy how he goes and gets testimonials. I got a $99 product, jeez I got to help him build a $900 product. So I got time for all that. So if you show up with your act together, you have real opportunity with those guys. Number three, if they have really good customers, they can't possibly satisfy their desire to consume. So they're always leaving money on the table and leaving them vulnerable to wandering off and getting seduced by somebody else. If they have good customers and they're grossly underserved, they're wandering off looking for stuff from other people. So you can help keep them connected for. This is a big piece of leverage when it exists, is when you are visibly and successfully selling direct to the consumers, or at least threatening to, in the same market that they are in. Now this always has to be said more elegantly than this, but this is, give me five bucks or I break your windows. And every week I'll come around and collect five bucks for insurance. This is, I'm going to sell to your customers with or without you. Your customers are going to buy from me whether you participate or they don't participate. The question here is not whether or not I'm going to get your customers, the question is whether you're going to get any of the money. Now that's as crass as you can put it, and it generally can't be said that crassly, but that's the message. So one of your greatest pieces of leverage in a defined marketplace where you want to get, I want to get him to let me at his customers, is if I am already visibly in that marketplace selling direct to his customers. Because if he's got a brain, the message is he's going to get my customers no matter what. And the smart ones get it right away because everybody knows a buyer is a buyer is a buyer is a buyer. So no matter what somebody gets interested in, the minute they get interested, if he goes to all the trouble to interest them in kettle bells, then the bastards start looking at dumbbells and every other damn fitness device. And in today's world, it's so easy for them once their interest is stimulated because they got to Google. So now they go Roman, right? And a buyer is a buyer is a buyer is a buyer, especially when they're in heat at the very beginning of their buying cycle. So if you are visibly in that guy's same space.
